snippets.title: OO scatter with labels and legend (recommended pattern)
snippets.code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
from sklearn.datasets import load_iris

X, y = load_iris(return_X_y=True)
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(6, 4))

for label in np.unique(y):
    mask = y == label
    ax.scatter(X[mask, 0], X[mask, 1], label=str(label), alpha=0.75, s=40)

ax.set_xlabel("Sepal length (cm)")
ax.set_ylabel("Sepal width (cm)")
ax.set_title("Iris — two features by species")
ax.legend(title="Species", frameon=False)
fig.tight_layout()
plt.show()
snippets.explanation: Canonical OO workflow: create fig/ax once, plot per series with label, set axis text, legend, tight_layout. Reusable in functions that return fig.

snippets.title: Train vs validation loss line plot
snippets.code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

epochs = range(1, len(train_loss) + 1)
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 4))

ax.plot(epochs, train_loss, label="Train loss", marker="o", markersize=3)
ax.plot(epochs, val_loss, label="Val loss", marker="o", markersize=3)

ax.set_xlabel("Epoch")
ax.set_ylabel("Loss")
ax.set_title("Training curve")
ax.legend()
ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
fig.tight_layout()
plt.show()
snippets.explanation: Shared axes, distinct labels, light grid for readability. Assumes train_loss and val_loss lists exist from your training loop.

snippets.title: Subplot grid of feature histograms
snippets.code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd

numeric_cols = df.select_dtypes("number").columns[:4]
fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(8, 6))
axes = axes.ravel()

for ax, col in zip(axes, numeric_cols):
    ax.hist(df[col].dropna(), bins=30, color="steelblue", edgecolor="white")
    ax.set_title(col)
    ax.set_ylabel("Count")

fig.suptitle("Feature distributions", y=1.02)
fig.tight_layout()
plt.show()
snippets.explanation: subplots returns a grid of Axes; ravel() flattens for easy iteration. suptitle for overall heading; per-ax titles for column names.

snippets.title: Save figure for a report
snippets.code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(6, 4))
ax.bar(["A", "B", "C"], [12, 7, 19])
ax.set_ylabel("Count")
fig.tight_layout()

fig.savefig("class_counts.png", dpi=150, bbox_inches="tight")
# plt.show()  # optional in scripts; skip when headless
snippets.explanation: savefig on the Figure object; dpi and bbox_inches='tight' avoid clipped labels. In scripts, omit show() when using non-interactive backend.
